799.title: Model-driven solutions with Active Knowledge Modelling (AKM)
799.summary: Research area: Active Knowledge Modeling (AKM) to advance enterprise modeling by capturing dynamic, reusable knowledge. Technologies: Mimris open-source modeling platform; potential integration of AI to support modeling tasks. Expected outcomes: Develop and evaluate an AKM-based solution for a specific problem domain, assessing its usability and usefulness. May also involve enhancing the Mimris modeling environment with AI-driven capabilities.

1103.title: Bioinspirert AI for å gjenskape/skape musikk
1103.summary: This project explores bio-inspired AI techniques, particularly evolutionary methods, for generating music. Students may choose any bio-inspired approach. A database of Mozart's music is available to evaluate if generated music shares similar stylistic characteristics. The expected outcome is a system that creates music with traits comparable to a given classical composer, using nature-inspired algorithms as the core methodology.

1472.title: FINANCE - genetic algorithm
1472.summary: This project applies genetic algorithms (GAs) to finance, focusing on portfolio optimization or another student-selected financial problem. A specific direction involves extending prior work on multi-objective differential evolution for Value at Risk (VaR) portfolio optimization. The student will define the precise financial objective and implement the chosen GA variant. Expected outcome is a working algorithmic solution for the selected financial optimization task, demonstrating GA effectiveness in that context.

1872.title: ChatBots - Dialog interfaces - Text / Speech
1872.summary: Upgrade Telebuster, a legacy dialog system for travel information, to 2026 standards by integrating GTFS and recent LLMs. Simplify architecture from Prolog-PHP-Java to two layers. Research focuses on NLP and dialog systems; expected outcome is a globally accessible, real-time schedule system with multilingual support via translation tools.

1875.title: Personalization - Natural Language Information Query (dialog)
1875.summary: Research area: Dialogue systems and personalization for travel information. Technologies: Speech recognition, GPS, maps, real-time data integration, and context memory. Expected outcomes: Develop a system that adapts to individual users by remembering past queries, improving satisfaction while addressing GDPR compliance for data retention.

2269.title: Problems in bioinformatics
2269.summary: Research area: Developing computational models to predict gene regulation changes in development and disease. Technologies: Machine learning (genetic programming, support vector machines), custom algorithms, statistical simulations, string and search algorithms. Expected outcomes: Predictive tools for analyzing biological data, such as identifying microRNA target sites, microRNA genes, and related proteins. Projects involve programming and algorithm development for biological data interpretation.

2518.title: Uninterrupted Instruction Supply in Servers
2518.summary: This project addresses front-end bottlenecks in server CPUs caused by large instruction footprints and branch mispredictions. It builds on the Boomerang prefetcher, which currently stops prefetching on any branch misprediction. The research will investigate techniques to enable continuous prefetching beyond mispredictions. Expected outcomes include higher instruction cache/BTB miss coverage and improved performance for modern server workloads like web search and video streaming. Key technologies involve branch prediction, instruction prefetching, and BTB management.

2557.title: The Strategic Value of Big Data Analytics
2557.summary: This master's thesis investigates how Norwegian companies leverage big data analytics to generate business value through a quantitative study. Conducted with the Big Data Observatory, the project involves developing research methodologies and analyzing quantitative data. Expected outcomes include insights into the tangible benefits and value capture mechanisms of big data investments, addressing ongoing debates about its strategic impact.

2560.title: What is the Business Value of Artificial Intelligence?
2560.summary: Research area: Organizational AI adoption and business value in private and public sectors. Technologies: AI, data analysis. Collaboration with Big Data Observatory for data collection. Expected outcomes: Assess organizational readiness for AI, quantify derived value, identify necessary adoption steps.

2561.title: Artificial Intelligence in Business: Uncovering challenges and obstacles of adoption.
2561.summary: Research area: Challenges and obstacles in adopting Artificial Intelligence within businesses, beyond just technological factors. Technologies: AI systems, big data analytics. Methods: Data collection and analysis in collaboration with the Big Data Observatory. Expected outcomes: Identification and analysis of key inhibiting factors (technological, organizational, human) to provide insights for overcoming barriers and enabling successful AI implementation.

2564.title: The potential of Artificial Intelligence for public administration
2564.summary: This master's project, in collaboration with SINTEF Digital, investigates AI adoption in public administration. It involves data collection, analysis, and reporting to assess current implementation status, identify key applications like fraud detection and chatbots, and evaluate realized value. The expected outcome is insights into AI's potential for enhancing public sector efficiency and decision-making.

2565.title: Human-Machine Symbiosis: How does decision-making change in the age of Big Data Analytics?
2565.summary: Exploring human decision-making in the Big Data era, this project examines cognitive overload and bias in analytics-based decisions. Collaborating with the Big Data Observatory, it uses eye-tracking and EEG to study neurophysiological responses. Expected to enhance understanding of human-machine symbiosis and inform better decision-support tools.

2566.title: What are the skills of the future: A look at how Artificial Intelligence and Analytics change the competences of computer science graduates
2566.summary: This project examines how Artificial Intelligence and big data analytics are transforming the essential skills for computer science graduates. In partnership with the Big Data Observatory, it uses focus groups and surveys with industry representatives to gather data. The outcome will be a detailed analysis of future competency needs and practical guidance for educational institutions to update their curricula accordingly.

2700.title: Computer Vision in Bad Weather conditions / Bad Visibility
2700.summary: Research focuses on robust computer vision for autonomous driving in adverse Nordic weather (rain, snow, spray). It combines classical signal processing and deep learning to design novel algorithms for detecting vehicles, road markings, barriers, and obstacles, explicitly modeling or learning from image degradations. The project leverages an existing bad-weather driving dataset and involves collaboration with a leading industrial partner in Germany, potentially including a guest stay. Expected outcome is improved perception systems for low-visibility conditions in automotive and marine applications.

2726.title: TrønderEnergi: Explainable time-series forecasting
2726.summary: This project addresses the need for explainable time-series forecasting in TrønderEnergi's operational systems. The research focuses on developing and evaluating prototype explanations for machine learning forecasts used by non-expert domain experts. Key technologies involve machine learning and time-series analysis applied to extensive datasets including consumption, grid loss, weather, and production data. The expected outcome is improved deployed forecasting systems through interpretable outputs that enable experts to understand and act upon predictions.

2737.title: Large-scale agile software develpment
2737.summary: Research Area: Adaptation and combination of agile practices with traditional methods for large-scale software development. Technologies: Agile frameworks (SAFe, LeSS, Spotify model, DAD), literature review, potential empirical study. Expected Outcomes: Insights into effective scaling methods, analysis of first vs. second generation approaches, practical guidelines informed by industry collaboration with partners like DNV GL and Equinor.

2747.title: Further development of a novel immune-inspired hybrid classification algorithm
2747.summary: This project extends a previously developed hybrid classification algorithm (MAIM) that combines immune system principles with an Island Model Genetic Algorithm. Building on promising initial results and a publication, the research will explore refinements and new extensions to this bio-inspired approach. The focus is on improving the algorithm's performance and capabilities for anomaly detection tasks. Expected outcomes include an enhanced version of MAIM and contributions to the field of immune-inspired computing.

2748.title: Bio-inspired techniques applied to remote sensing with hyperspectral data
2748.summary: This project applies bio-inspired AI techniques to hyperspectral remote sensing data for environmental monitoring. Key methods include evolutionary algorithms (NSGA-II) and particle swarm optimization (PSO-FCM) for unsupervised band selection, with superpixels used to improve efficiency. The research addresses high data dimensionality, aiming to enhance classification performance and processing efficiency for analyzing vegetation, water, and land-use changes. Students can continue existing work or propose new bio-inspired approaches.

2753.title: Particle Swarm Optimisation as a modelling tool for Climate Change
2753.summary:
Research Area: Applying Particle Swarm Optimisation (PSO) to model social dynamics and individual behaviour change in the context of climate change mitigation.

Technologies: Development of a PSO-based simulation environment where particles represent individuals within a society.

Expected Outcomes: A modeling tool to investigate the factors and influences that incentivise individuals to increase personal environmental contributions or sacrifices for the long-term benefit of future populations.

2867.title: Artificial Intelligence in the Healthcare sector
2867.summary: This project examines the impact of AI and advanced analytics on healthcare practitioners' roles. It will analyze how technologies like machine learning are transforming tasks in diagnostics and administration, evaluating both the benefits (e.g., increased efficiency) and the negative consequences (e.g., job displacement) for workers such as doctors and nurses.

2890.title: Application of AI in Norwegian Organization
2890.summary: Research area: AI adoption in Norwegian public sector organizations. Methods: Analysis of existing quantitative and qualitative survey data from longitudinal studies, combined with a literature review. Technologies: Focus on AI solution implementation. Expected outcomes: New knowledge on mechanisms affecting resource utilization during AI adoption; an English-language report intended as a basis for scientific publications.

2907.title: Organizational decision-making in the age of AI
2907.summary: Research area: Organizational decision-making with AI. Technologies: AI systems for automation and augmentation in processes like credit scoring and forecasting. Expected outcomes: Empirical insights into the effects and processes of transitioning to AI-based decision-making structures in organizations.

2908.title: Responsible AI in organizations
2908.summary: Research area: Organizational implementation of Responsible AI. Technologies: AI governance frameworks, lifecycle monitoring processes, and responsibility indicators. Expected outcomes: Analysis of organizational structures and processes for attaining responsible AI; assessment of impacts including potential gains, effects on stakeholder perception, and constraints on technology adoption.

2909.title: Exploring the black box of AI Governance
2909.summary:
Research Area: Organizational implementation of AI governance.  
Technologies: Focus is on governance frameworks, not specific AI tech.  
Expected Outcomes: Understand how organizations develop, deploy, and measure AI governance practices, including influencing factors and effects on business value and project performance.

2941.title: Improving Branch Prediction Accuracy for Cloud Applications
2941.summary: Research area: Computer architecture, focusing on branch prediction for cloud workloads with large instruction footprints. Technologies: Branch Target Buffer (BTB) design and branch prediction algorithms. Expected outcomes: 1) Investigate and implement improved BTB replacement policies to reduce miss rates caused by gigantic branch working sets. 2) Develop a categorization scheme for branches based on their performance impact to optimize the branch predictor, prioritizing accuracy for the most costly mispredictions. Goal is to enhance fetch stage efficiency and overall performance for cloud applications.

2948.title: GPU-accelerated graph management
2948.summary: This project studies GPU-accelerated graph analytics to computationally expensive operations. The core challenge is efficiently partitioning and executing graph queries using combined CPU/GPU resources. Tasks involve surveying prior work, identifying suitable GPU-friendly queries, developing supporting algorithms or indexes, and evaluating performance on large datasets. The expected outcome is optimized graph query/mine solutions leveraging GPU parallelism. Prerequisites are strong programming skills and interest in algorithms.

3064.title: Visual Simultaneous Localization and Mapping (VSLAM) for an Underwater Snake Robot (H2025-V2026)
3064.summary: This project develops Visual SLAM (VSLAM) algorithms specifically for an underwater snake robot, addressing challenges like turbid water, moving illumination, and visual clutter. The work involves experimenting with state-of-the-art geometric and machine learning approaches for robust keypoint tracking, state estimation, and optimization. Using real mission footage and a high-fidelity simulator with ground truth, the focus is adaptable to the student's interests, potentially covering IMU fusion, depth estimation, or pose graph optimization. The expected outcome is a tuned VSLAM system capable of reliable localization and mapping in demanding underwater environments.

3084.title: Numerical Contours
3084.summary: This project develops a scalable annotator for numerical and temporal expressions in large text collections like Wikipedia. Key research questions include annotating numerical quantities with entities, modeling units for easy conversion, and ensuring scalability. It spans databases, information retrieval, and natural language processing. The expected outcome is a system that can reliably extract, normalize, and link numerical data associated with entities across vast document sets.

3085.title: Semantic Text Indexing
3085.summary: This project investigates scalable semantic text indexing to enhance search for underspecified queries. It explores alternative semantic embedding methods beyond blackbox deep learning (e.g., word2vec), focusing on efficient indexing and matching across millions of documents. Research intersects databases, information retrieval, and natural language processing, aiming to improve relevance and performance in large-scale text retrieval systems.

3086.title: Event Mining and Linking
3086.summary: This project focuses on mining events from large-scale news archives and linking them into coherent storylines for user comprehension. It combines data mining, natural language processing, information retrieval, and database technologies to extract and connect contextual information. The expected outcome is a system that automatically weaves related events into narrative structures, enhancing the understanding of specialized or minute news topics.

3087.title: Learning Indexing Patterns from Annotated Document Collections
3087.summary: This project explores improving document retrieval from collections annotated with entities, time, and numbers using NLP tools (e.g., Stanford CoreNLP). It focuses on designing efficient database indexing strategies across research areas of databases, information retrieval, data mining, and natural language processing. The expected outcome is a novel indexing approach that significantly speeds up queries filtering documents by specific time periods, entity categories, and numerical ranges.

3089.title: Text Driven Analytics and Visualization
3089.summary: This project involves designing a system that uses scalable information extraction to process large volumes of web documents with annotations like entities and time. Focusing on databases, information retrieval, and data mining, it aims to generate interactive visualizations and enable user-driven text analytics. The expected outcome is a tool for deriving insights from annotated text data through visual exploration.

3106.title: Interesting AI project
3106.summary: This proposal outlines a flexible AI research project. The specific research area, technologies, and expected outcomes are not defined; they will be determined through collaboration between the student and Prof. Ole Jakob Mengshoel, based on mutual interests and the professor's prior research. The project requires direct email contact to establish this shared understanding before a match is made. No concrete technical details are provided in the description.

3124.title: An immune system approach to fake News Classification
3124.summary: This project applies artificial immune systems (AIS), inspired by biological immune responses, to fake news classification. Students will extend and refine existing AIS approaches, with options to develop fine-grained datasets, explore recognition regions, or optimize performance. Expected outcomes include advanced AIS models for misinformation detection and contributions to AIS research in machine learning.

3130.title: VRP with trucks and drones for Health Service package delivery
3130.summary: This project focuses on solving the Vehicle Routing Problem with Drones (VRPD) for delivering time-critical medical packages (e.g., blood, vaccines) to remote or dense urban areas. It involves developing optimization models for combined truck-drone logistics. Key technologies include biological-inspired algorithms such as Genetic Algorithms, Multi-objective Optimization, and Particle Swarm Optimization. The expected outcome is an efficient, adaptive routing solution to improve last-mile delivery cost, speed, and reliability in dynamic, uncertain environments.

3136.title: *NEW*: Attention Beyond the Visible: Transformer Models for Pattern Inference Under Occlusion (2025-2026)
3136.summary: Research Area: Developing Transformer models to infer partially occluded patterns in visual or symbolic data, focusing on how attention mechanisms handle uncertainty. Technologies: Transformer architectures, self-attention analysis, mutual information, rate-distortion theory, vector superposition coding. Expected Outcomes: Design synthetic occlusion tasks, analyze internal attention maps to detect latent structure, and theoretically bound reconstruction conditions. Conducted in cooperation with Jotun.

3158.title: Managing big data volumes
3158.summary: Research area: Parallel data insertion methods for big data. Focus on scaling parallel insertion in traditional index structures (B-trees, R-trees) and handling sequential log-structured data like trajectories. Applications include satellite geospatial data and GPS trajectory storage. Expected outcomes involve developing efficient parallel insertion techniques to overcome scalability limitations in current parallel database architectures, particularly those with shared logs.

3159.title: Merging of offline databases
3159.summary: Research area: Distributed systems and offline-first data synchronization for scenarios like disaster response. Involves evaluating and potentially implementing techniques such as CRDTs, mergeable data structures, and operational transformation to reconcile independently updated databases after a disconnection period. Expected outcome is a solution or comparative analysis for reliable offline merging.

3161.title: NoSQL/NewSQL (scalable)  databases
3161.summary: This master's project explores scalable NoSQL and NewSQL databases, with potential research topics including transaction models, multi-dimensional indexes, autotuning, automatic upgrading, autoscaling, and storage/indexing. Students are encouraged to propose original ideas or tailor projects to a preferred database. Expected outcomes center on innovations enhancing database scalability, automation, and performance optimization.

3162.title: Similarity of trajectories
3162.summary: The project focuses on trajectory similarity algorithms for applications like ride sharing and mapping. It involves designing an efficient indexing method, such as Locality Sensitive Hashing (LSH), to pre-filter data before applying computationally heavy algorithms like Dynamic Time Warping. Building on previous parameter optimizations for LSH, the expected outcome is a tailored indexing strategy that enhances performance for diverse trajectory datasets.

3163.title: AI for sustainable business models
3163.summary: This project investigates how Artificial Intelligence (AI) can enable firms to transition to Sustainable Business Models (SBMs) by providing data-driven insights for complex decision-making. It addresses a gap in understanding the mechanisms linking AI to sustainable value creation and competitive performance. The research will synthesize existing knowledge through a literature review to answer: 1) how companies leverage AI for SBMs, and 2) how to conceptualize the relationship between AI, SBM, and performance. Expected outcomes include a comprehensive mapping of the field and a conceptual framework to guide firms in accelerating their sustainable transition using AI.

3165.title: AI for understanding marine ecosystems by using underwater acoustic data
3165.summary: This project applies AI, specifically transformer models, to analyze large-scale underwater acoustic data from ocean observatories. It aims to classify and understand complex sound sources (biophony and anthrophony) within marine ecosystems. The research addresses challenges like data variability and noise. The work involves handling big data streams and will be conducted in collaboration with NTNU’s Department of Biology, building on previous student projects. Expected outcomes include effective models for acoustic event classification and analysis to improve marine ecosystem monitoring.

3182.title: Eye-tracking and training novice programmers  (multiple projects)
3182.summary: Research area: Using eye-tracking and dialogue to train novice programmers in debugging via expert gaze mapping. Technologies: Python/Java for developing a gaze-enabled feedback tool. Expected outcomes: Implement and evaluate a system that provides feedback based on expert gaze and dialogue, with usability and effectiveness assessed through user studies.

3183.title: AI-Based gaze-aware feedback system for programming  (multiple projects)
3183.summary: This project develops an AI-based real-time feedback system for programming education, using eye-tracking and IDE log data to assist students with medium-size software tasks. The student will design and implement the tool in Python, conduct usability and effectiveness user studies, and analyze results. Key technologies include machine learning, system design, and Python programming. Expected outcomes are a functional gaze-aware feedback tool and empirical evaluations of its impact on learning.

3187.title: Artificial Intelligence and pair programming (multiple projects)
3187.summary: Research area: AI for predicting pair programming performance using multi-sensor data. Technologies: Eye-tracking, EEG, heart rate, facial expressions, Python, machine/deep learning. Expected outcomes: Collect sensor data during pair programming, develop prediction algorithms from interaction features, compare methods, and complete thesis.

3188.title: Artificial Intelligence and Biometrics in Education (multiple projects)
3188.summary: This project explores AI applications in education using biometric sensors (eye-tracking, EEG, heart rate, skin temperature, facial expressions) to collect multimodal student data. The goal is to combine these data sources to better predict student behavior and academic performance, improving learning experiences. The work involves system design, machine learning, and hands-on development. Required skills include Python/Java programming and an interest in experimentation.

3245.title: Learning software organisations
3245.summary: Research area: Organisational learning and knowledge management in software engineering, emphasizing team-level over individual-level learning. Technologies: Not explicitly specified; relies on literature review and empirical methods. Expected outcomes: Comprehensive survey of existing research and potential empirical study in a Norwegian software environment to identify effective learning practices, with the student proposing or being assigned a case.

3261.title: Uncertainty Quantification in Detection and Classification Problems
3261.summary: This project focuses on improving deep learning classifiers to detect when input data is unfamiliar or distorted, preventing confidently wrong predictions in critical applications. The student will review and implement uncertainty-aware methods (e.g., Bayesian deep learning, evidential deep learning) and test them on a boat image dataset from DNV plus standard benchmarks (CIFAR-10/100, RETINA). Tests will involve out-of-distribution samples and manipulated images (blur, rotation, overlays). The expected outcome is models that can quantify meaningful uncertainty, distinguish epistemic from aleatoric uncertainty, and reliably signal "I don't know" for high-risk decision support.

3266.title: Biometrics and Neuro-conceptualization
3266.summary: This project investigates how the brain processes visual conceptual models (e.g., UML, BPMN) using biometric data (heart rate, EEG, eye-tracking). It combines neuroscience, cognitive psychology, and information systems research. The student will review existing literature in NeuroIS and NeuroSE, then design and conduct experiments to study neural processing during model comprehension, individual differences, and optimal modeling approaches. The thesis, written in English, is expected to yield material for a scientific publication.

3285.title: Sustainability by design
3285.summary: This project investigates integrating sustainability requirements into agile software development. Research focuses on eliciting, expressing, and balancing these requirements with traditional ones, and evaluating their long-term adherence. The methodology involves an initial structured literature review followed by evaluating existing or new techniques for handling sustainability in agile contexts. Expected outcomes include practical frameworks or guidelines to achieve sustainability by design, supporting the work of NTNU's Center for Sustainable ICT (CESICT) and the national goforIT network.

3293.title: Predictive Maintenance in Fish Farming
3293.summary: This project develops predictive maintenance methods for fish farming operations, such as cleaning feeding systems, to optimize scheduling and reduce unnecessary downtime. It involves identifying high-impact maintenance tasks and using data analytics and machine learning on industrial IoT sensor data to predict when maintenance is truly needed. The expected outcome is a model that reliably forecasts maintenance requirements, improving efficiency and sustainability in aquaculture. The work is done in collaboration with Piscada, which provides an industrial cloud-based IoT platform for deployment.

3294.title: Tag inference
3294.summary: This project focuses on developing a machine learning model to classify building equipment (tags) from time series sensor data. The student will preprocess high-frequency, multi-building time series data and train a classifier using a provided labeled dataset. Key challenges include handling varying operational patterns across buildings and potentially using clustering to refine equipment classes. The outcome is a functional tag inference model, with the project conducted in collaboration with Piscada, using their anonymized building automation data.

3295.title: Predictive Maintenance in Building Automation & HVAC Systems
3295.summary: This project focuses on developing predictive maintenance methods for Building Automation & HVAC systems to optimize maintenance schedules and reduce unnecessary replacements. Technologies involved include IoT sensor data, cloud platforms, and data analytics. The expected outcome is a model to reliably predict maintenance needs for selected operations, improving efficiency and sustainability. Collaborating with Piscada, a PropTech Industrial IoT company, the project aims to enhance their platform with predictive insights for commercial buildings.

3305.title: [NorwAI] Neural Machine Translation in Written Norwegian - Bokmål and Nynorsk - with Generative Pre-trained Transformer
3305.summary: This project develops a GPT-based neural machine translation model for Bokmål and Nynorsk, two low-resource Norwegian written forms. Using a pre-trained 318M parameter Norwegian GPT-2 model and a small parallel corpus (200k sentence pairs), it investigates efficient fine-tuning methods. Key challenges include handling named entities, long text translation, and reducing the computational cost of fine-tuning large models. The expected outcome is an effective translation system for this specific language pair with limited training data.

3328.title: [ExerGames] Play to get fit 2024/2025
3328.summary: This project explores exergame design, balancing gameplay fun with effective physical exercise. Research involves studying exergame theory and motivational mechanics. The technical phase develops a prototype using unspecified technologies, followed by user evaluation testing. The expected outcome is a functional exergame prototype demonstrating the balance between entertainment and fitness, supported by evaluation data.

3332.title: Analysing the Effectiveness of Last Level (Data) Cache for Cloud Applications
3332.summary: This project analyzes the effectiveness of last-level caches (LLCs) for cloud applications like web search and video streaming. It will use hardware simulation to evaluate different cache configurations, sizes, and eviction policies on representative cloud workloads. The goal is to identify performance bottlenecks and propose architectural modifications to reduce wasted energy, chip area, and processing cycles caused by inefficient LLCs in modern server processors. The research requires expertise in system architecture and simulation setup.

3383.title: Analyzing the Effectiveness of Last Level Cache for Cloud Applications
3383.summary: This project investigates the effectiveness of last-level caches (LLCs) for cloud workloads like web search and video streaming. Using simulation, it will analyze how different LLC sizes, hierarchies, and eviction policies impact performance, energy use, and area. The goal is to identify configuration bottlenecks where LLCs cause net overhead and to propose optimizations for future processor designs targeted at cloud applications, aiming to reduce wasted resources.

3386.title: A bio-inspried AI approach to Music recommendation
3386.summary: Research area: Music recommendation systems using bio-inspired AI techniques. Technologies: Immune system algorithms (e.g., AIRS), content-based and collaborative filtering, multiobjective optimization. Expected outcomes: Extend immune-based approaches for music recommendation, explore hybrid content/collaborative methods, and integrate user preference modeling into decision-making frameworks. Students can choose specific bio-inspired techniques and apply them to music or other recommendation domains.

3387.title: Evolving personalised training programs
3387.summary: Research area: Preventing metabolic syndrome through personalized health interventions. Technology: Genetic algorithms. Expected outcome: An adaptive system that generates continuous, dynamic training programs which evolve in response to a patient's changing health status, aiming for long-term disease prevention.

3398.title: Hybrid co-design toolkits
3398.summary: This project explores adapting traditional co-design methods and toolkits for hybrid (in-person/digital) workshops, accelerated by the COVID-19 pandemic. It involves researching existing strategies, developing a comparative framework, and prototyping new hybrid toolkits that blend digital platforms (e.g., Zoom, Miro) with physical artifacts. The expected outcome is a set of validated hybrid co-design prototypes to facilitate inclusive participation across distributed teams.

3399.title: Code re-use and binary diffing as tools for reverse engineering of firmware from unknown instruction set architectures
3399.summary: This project explores using binary diffing and code re-use analysis to reverse engineer proprietary instruction set architectures (ISAs) from multiple firmware versions. Using the Shimano STePS electric bicycle firmware as a case study, it will infer subroutines, data structures, and ISA characteristics like instruction formats, opcodes, and register layouts by analyzing changes between firmware releases and correlating them with changelog metadata. The expected outcome is a methodology for deducing unknown ISA details from sequential binary samples and sparse documentation.

3408.title: Computational nanosystems: Toward real word tasks
3408.summary: This project explores using artificial spin ice (ASI), a system of coupled nanomagnets, as a novel computational substrate for reservoir computing. The research focuses on adapting theoretical findings to solve real-world tasks: MNIST handwritten digit recognition and spoken digit recognition. Work involves simulations with the open-source flatspin simulator to design efficient input encodings, define capable ASI reservoirs, and develop readout strategies. It is part of the SOCRATES and SpinENGIN projects investigating nanomagnet ensembles for unconventional, bio-inspired computing.

3411.title: Effects of Interpreted Languages on Microarchitectural Performance
3411.summary:
Research Area: Microarchitectural performance effects of interpreted languages (Python, JavaScript, PHP) in cloud/FaaS workloads.  
Technologies: Hardware performance analysis, cache hierarchy, branch prediction, prefetching; custom tool development to separate interpreter code from program data in caches.  
Expected Outcomes: New measurement tool; analysis of front-end (cache usage, prefetching, branch prediction) and back-end impacts; proposals for hardware/software optimizations to improve performance and energy efficiency for interpreted workloads.

3412.title: Application of artificial intelligence-based image soft-sensor to high temperature property study
3412.summary: This project develops an AI-based image soft-sensor using machine learning and deep neural networks to automate the analysis of high-temperature material properties from sessile drop experiment images/videos. It focuses on data pre-processing, including super-resolution techniques, and implements the soft-sensor at SINTEF's wetting lab to improve efficiency and accuracy over manual evaluation. Supervised by NTNU and SINTEF, it addresses challenges in real-time material characterization.

3427.title: Data science - in practice
3427.summary: This project empirically studies conditions for applying data science in organizational decision-making, using cases from energy and healthcare. It focuses on enhancing algorithm accountability and explainability (XAI) with data-driven techniques. Expected outcomes include insights to bridge the gap between data science theory and practical implementation, funded by the Norwegian Research Council via SFI NorwAI.

3432.title: Open data - from vision to reality?
3432.summary: This project investigates the gap between open data visions and practical adoption, using the Open Data Subsurface Universe (OSDU) platform as a case study. The research will analyze the social, practical, and institutional conditions necessary for OSDU's success in transitioning from fossil energy to renewables and CCS. Key technologies include the OSDU data framework and analytics for geo-located assets. Students will work with industry partners Equinor and Aker BP to produce an analysis of implementation barriers and enablers.

3433.title: Learned index and updates
3433.summary: This project focuses on the research area of learned indexes, specifically addressing update handling and multi-dimensional extensions. The key technologies involve machine learning models for data indexing. Expected outcomes include a survey of current methods for managing inserts and updates in learned indexes, proposing improvements for high-load scenarios, and investigating open research problems for multi-dimensional (e.g., R-tree-like) and string indexes, which are noted as particularly challenging.

3528.title: *NEW* Investigations on Situation Awareness Systems for an Autonomous Ferry
3528.summary: This project investigates AI-based Situation Awareness Systems for autonomous ferries, focusing on sensor data fusion to build dynamic environmental models for safe navigation, collision avoidance, and docking. Conducted with industrial partners, it offers flexibility to explore machine learning or classical statistical methods, depending on student interest. It builds on prior work in the Maritime Perception Cluster and emphasizes practical AI applications in maritime autonomy.

3529.title: Learning analytics and AI in Education in Norway
3529.summary: This project examines learning analytics and AI in education in Norway, analyzing national datasets and potentially collecting primary data via questionnaires or interviews. It aims to identify existing systems, their practical implementation, and associated challenges and opportunities, with emphasis on data policies and ethical practices. Suitable for students with data analysis and research skills, no programming required.

3530.title: Reinforcement learning for multi-agent simulated autonomous driving
3530.summary: This project develops a reinforcement learning-based multi-agent system for autonomous driving in urban environments. Research focuses on creating self-steering algorithms for simulated cars. The work can be conducted using either the realistic CARLA driving simulator or physical LIMO robot platforms. The adaptable scope allows alignment with student interests in simulation or real-world robotics. Expected outcomes include a functional multi-agent reinforcement learning scheme applicable to cooperative autonomous driving scenarios.

3532.title: Empowering Children’s STEM Learning through Embodied Interaction and GenAI capabilities
3532.summary: This project investigates combining motion-based interaction (using sensors for full-body gestures) and generative AI (LLMs/multimodal LLMs) to enhance children's STEM learning, focusing on math. It employs multimodal learning analytics (MMLA) to develop an adaptive system that suggests learning activities based on real-time motion data. The student will design, implement (using Python/C#/JavaScript), and evaluate technological interfaces that provide dynamic, AI-driven feedback. Required: data science background, LLM integration experience, and strong backend programming skills.

3535.title: Using LLMs and conversational agents to support children’s learning
3535.summary: Research area: Enhancing children's science learning in informal settings (science centers/museums) using AI. Technologies: Mobile application development integrating Large Language Models (LLMs) and multimodal LLMs (e.g., CodeLLama) with conversational agents; front-end programming (JavaScript, CSS). Expected outcomes: Develop and evaluate an interactive app allowing children to ask questions, empirically test its impact on engagement and learning through user studies, and produce a thesis analyzing the results.

3536.title: Online Learning Platform – Enhancing Personalization with Self-Regulated Learning
3536.summary: This project enhances Progresso, an online learning platform, by implementing a Self-Regulated Learning (SRL) module to improve student autonomy and engagement. You will develop features for goal setting, progress monitoring, and reflection using React/Next.js (frontend), Node.js/Prisma (backend), and Firebase (database). A user study will evaluate the module's impact on learning outcomes. The expected outcome is a validated SRL integration that increases learning efficiency and retention.

3540.title: Education in the era of generative AI: Understanding the potential benefits of LLMs in teaching and learning
3540.summary: Research Area: Impact of generative AI (LLMs like ChatGPT) on education. Technologies: Large Language Models (LLMs), specifically ChatGPT. Method: Case study implementation to investigate opportunities and challenges. Expected Outcomes: Analysis of AI's potential to improve teaching and learning, with practical recommendations for safe and constructive integration of generative AI tools in educational settings to support students.

3541.title: Evolutionary Algorithms with Applications
3541.summary: This project studies evolutionary algorithms (EAs) within AI/ML, focusing on their theoretical analysis and practical improvement. Research questions include enhancing EAs by combining heuristics and adaptive methods, applying Markov chain analysis and distributed computing to reduce computational costs in evolutionary machine learning, and exploring connections between parametric Markov chains and trustworthy AI. Key technologies involve stochastic search, Markov chains, and high-performance computing. Expected outcomes are more efficient EA-based methods for complex datasets and bridging formal methods with AI applications.

3542.title: Human-Centered AI technologies for teaching and learning
3542.summary: Research area: Human-Centered AI in education, emphasizing Hybrid Intelligence for teacher-AI collaboration. Technologies: Co-design methods, six levels of automation model, and Learn Lab's EdTech platforms with AI for personalized feedback. Expected outcomes: Design learning artefacts illustrating human-AI synergy, articulate control transitions, and enhance educational efficiency, equity, and teacher time savings.

3612.title: Understanding the execution behavior of high-level productivity languages
3612.summary: This project investigates the hardware execution behavior of high-level languages (e.g., Python, JavaScript) to address a research gap caused by traditional focus on compiled languages like C++. It involves designing a tool to distinguish application code from data in cache lines. The research will analyze resulting impacts on processor front-end and back-end components. The expected outcome is proposing novel methods to enhance both performance and energy efficiency for these prevalent workloads.

3622.title: Unsupervised learning for automatically annotating eye tracking data
3622.summary: Research area: automating the categorization of eye movements. Technologies: unsupervised learning, data augmentation, analysis of existing open annotated eye-tracking datasets. Expected outcomes: assess dataset quality, develop unsupervised methods to improve automatic annotation accuracy, compare results against existing annotations, and explore application to VR headsets where head movement adds complexity.

3624.title: VR environment for experiment design in medical research
3624.summary: This project develops a VR environment for medical research experiment design, focusing on integrating synchronized EEG and eye-tracking data. Students will assess the feasibility of this integrated system regarding accuracy and usability. Additionally, they will expand the VR environment by implementing tests for visual neglect, replacing traditional pen-and-paper assessments. This aims to demonstrate the system's usefulness while enabling novel data collection via sensors. The results will serve as a foundation for future research.

3629.title: IMTEL - Innovative education at the intersection of XR and ChatGPT
3629.summary: This project explores combining XR and generative AI to innovate educational tools at NTNU. Key technologies involve virtual/augmented reality and AI like ChatGPT for virtual teaching assistants and Magic3D for 3D content creation. Expected outcomes include developing interactive virtual classrooms with 24/7 AI support and enabling teachers and students without programming skills to rapidly generate 3D educational materials.

3636.title: Personalized Health
3636.summary: Master's project in Personalized Health, using recommender systems to predict optimized medical interventions for individuals. Involves AI, Machine Learning, and Recommender Systems technologies. Expected to implement a system for a specific domain like nutrition or exercise and create a demonstrator, requiring strong programming skills.

3646.title: Open-ended evolution
3646.summary: This project investigates open-ended evolution (OEE), aiming to replicate biology's endless novelty generation. It builds on the Paired Open-Ended Trailblazer (POET) algorithm, which coevolves increasingly difficult problems and solutions. A recent hybrid approach combined POET with Neuroevolution of Augmented Topologies (NEAT). An ablation study tested the necessity of POET's minimal criterion and transfer mechanisms in challenging maze-like environments with adversaries. The project offers extensions: further developing this hybrid algorithm or exploring alternative OEE approaches. Key technologies include coevolution, neuroevolution, and algorithmic ablation studies.

3650.title: Generating simulations that match data ​
3650.summary: This project focuses on simulation-based inference within hybrid AI, combining machine learning and physical models. It addresses Bayesian inference for complex industrial systems without analytic likelihoods, using simulations from wind turbine and compressor use cases. The applied track integrates commercial simulation software with real data to accelerate model parameter identification and uncertainty quantification. The theoretical track develops constrained machine learning models for probability distributions. Key outcomes include advancing likelihood-free inference methods, uncertainty quantification, and applying them to predictive maintenance and virtual flow metering.

3654.title: ​Generating synthetic data​ (Sparebank1 SMN)
3654.summary: Research area: Synthetic data generation for banking to enable data sharing and cloud computing while preserving privacy. Technologies: GANs, statistical modeling, potentially LLMs for text. Two approaches: synthesizing aggregated analytical tables or relational multi-table data (transactions, customers, products). Expected outcome: A synthetic banking dataset that maintains the original data's statistical relationships and inferential utility without containing any real personal information, facilitating open research and external compute resource usage.

3657.title: Classifying animals in the wild
3657.summary: Research area: wildlife image classification in complex natural environments. Technologies: deep learning models trained on large camera trap datasets (EE, SW, SS, CC, NINA). Expected outcome: a system that simultaneously categorizes foreground animal species (e.g., elk, deer) and background surroundings (e.g., snow, grass, trees) from challenging in-the-wild images.

3666.title: Exploring the design space of convolutional neural networks on edge systems
3666.summary: This project investigates the design space of convolutional neural networks (CNNs) for deployment on resource-constrained edge systems, specifically NVIDIA Jetson platforms. It will study how varying CNN width, depth, and resolution impacts key metrics: accuracy, energy consumption, and latency. The expected outcome is to define an effective design space for CNNs on edge devices and derive insights for designing efficient and effective models suited to edge deployment.

3699.title: Aneo: Ensambles for probabilistic forecasts of wind power production
3699.summary:
Research Area: Probabilistic wind power forecasting using weather ensemble data to improve accuracy and quantify uncertainty.

Technologies: Machine learning models applied to weather ensemble scenarios and operational wind farm data.

Expected Outcomes: Development and evaluation of models that utilize multiple weather forecast ensemble members to produce probabilistic day-ahead wind power forecasts, enhancing prediction reliability for grid balancing.

3701.title: Aneo: Predictive maintenance of cooling systems in retail stores
3701.summary: This project focuses on predictive maintenance for retail cooling systems using AI. The research involves analyzing historical fault data and sensor measurements (temperature, pressure, energy consumption) to build machine learning models that forecast equipment failures and time-to-failure. Key tasks include data preparation, model development, experimental evaluation, and result analysis. Expected outcomes are a functional predictive model and insights to optimize maintenance scheduling, reduce downtime, and manage parts inventory. The work is conducted with Aneo, a renewable energy company, using provided cloud infrastructure and private CSV data.

3702.title: Aneo: Why do wind turbines fail?
3702.summary: Research area: automating root-cause analysis for early wind turbine component failures using vibration and SCADA data. Technologies: machine learning/AI for feature extraction and anomaly detection on high-frequency vibration data and system statistics. Expected outcome: a programmable tool to automate identification of failure causes, supporting maintenance decisions despite limited labeled failure cases, leveraging expert fault descriptions. Project involves data investigation, literature review, method testing, and implementation within Aneo's AI energy systems framework.

3724.title: Multiplayer game development using Web3 Technologies
3724.summary: This project involves developing a multiplayer Blackjack game integrating traditional web (Spring Boot backend, any frontend framework) with Web3 technologies (EVM smart contracts in Solidity, web3.js). Key research areas include self-sovereign identities, scalability, account abstraction, Play2Earn mechanics, cross-chain interoperability, and user onboarding. The expected outcome is a functional game where players use blockchain wallets for accounts, transfer tokens to game accounts, have assets locked during play, and receive automated post-game settlements. It also explores architectural best practices for Web3 game platforms.

3728.title: AI for eyesight
3728.summary: Research area: AI-driven diagnostics for vision issues and spectacle prescription. Technologies: computer vision algorithms. Partnership: International startup developing novel eye care solutions. Focus: Industrial application and scalable impact for low-resource settings (Third World). Expected outcome: A deployable diagnostic tool to improve accessibility of eye care.

3734.title: Underwater object detection and classification
3734.summary: This project develops a machine learning system for underwater object detection and classification using sonar data and imagery. It addresses challenges like low visibility and varying conditions to accurately identify submerged objects such as marine life, archaeological sites, and infrastructure, supporting ocean conservation and sustainable management.

3736.title: Federated Learning for Privacy-Preserving Medical Image Analysis: A Collaborative Approach
3736.summary: This project investigates federated learning for privacy-preserving medical image analysis. It employs a decentralized machine learning approach where multiple medical institutions collaboratively train a shared model without exchanging raw patient data, using only model updates. Technologies focus on federated learning frameworks. Expected outcomes include a diagnostic model for tasks like disease detection or image segmentation, ensuring patient privacy and minimizing data breach risks in healthcare.

3737.title: FireWatch: Advanced Techniques for Wildfire Detection and Early Warning Systems
3737.summary: Research Area: Wildfire detection and early warning systems using AI and computer vision. Technologies: Remote sensing data, sensor networks, machine learning, artificial intelligence. Expected Outcomes: A robust, proactive system for early wildfire detection through accurate signature identification, optimized real-time processing, and efficient alert mechanisms to minimize impact on lives and the environment.

3738.title: Auto-Encoding Knowledge Graph for Unsupervised Medical Report Generation: Bridging Medical Imaging and Natural Language Processing
3738.summary: Research area combines medical imaging and NLP using autoencoders to learn compact representations of images and metadata. A knowledge graph structures relationships between medical concepts. Expected outcome: unsupervised generation of coherent medical reports by transforming the graph into natural language narratives describing images and findings.

3739.title: Beyond contemporary AI: A neural symbolic approach for anomaly detection in medical data
3739.summary: Neural-symbolic hybrid method for anomaly detection in medical data, combining neural networks for pattern learning and symbolic reasoning for explanation and context. Expected to deliver an innovative, interpretable system improving detection accuracy and trust in medical AI.

3741.title: Development of ´Learn and Earn/Learn to Care’ like gaming platform using mix of Web3 and traditional gaming Technologies
3741.summary: This project involves developing a hybrid multiplayer gaming platform combining Web3 and traditional technologies, centered on a "Learn and Earn" mechanic where players solve puzzles or answer questions to earn redeemable digital assets. The student must propose a specific game type. Key technologies include smart contracts and digital asset management. Expected outcomes are a functional platform with reward systems, user-generated content support, and research comparing architectural principles, user onboarding challenges, scalability, and security implications of public smart contracts in gaming.

3747.title: Exciting topics in HPC & Parallel Computing
3747.summary: This open-ended project invites students to propose their own research topics within HPC and parallel computing, including parallel algorithms, GPU/HPC system testing (e.g., NVIDIA B100s), quantum computing simulations, cloud technologies, or educational tool development. The focus is on feasibility assessment for a fall project or master's thesis. Students must contact Prof. Anne Elster to discuss their specific idea. Expected outcomes are a research project or thesis on the approved topic.

3748.title: Automated Single-Cell Type Annotation for Salmon Skin Disease Prevention
3748.summary: This project develops an automated machine learning tool for annotating single-cell types in salmon genomic samples to address skin disease in aquaculture. Using Python and ML libraries, it aims to replace manual annotation, speeding up analysis and aiding disease prevention. Collaboration with University of Stirling provides real-world datasets and interdisciplinary experience.

3753.title: Machine learning for nanomagnetic computing systems
3753.summary: This project applies machine learning to optimize the design of Artificial Spin Ice (ASI) systems for neuromorphic computing. Using the flatspin simulator integrated with PyTorch, it aims to tune nanomagnet placements for target functions like pattern recognition. The interdisciplinary work combines physics, AI, and computing to develop ultra-low-power, non-von Neumann architectures based on emergent magnetic phenomena.

3754.title: Astroid clocking of nanomagnetic computing systems
3754.summary: This project explores astroid clocking protocols to control the dynamic behavior of artificial spin ice (ASI) systems for ultra-low-power neuromorphic computing. Using the flatspin simulator, it aims to develop new clocking methods, apply them to diverse ASI types, and enhance step-wise control over nanomagnet state evolution. Expected outcomes include advanced protocols enabling pausable, spatially controlled computations in magnetic metamaterials, advancing unconventional computing substrates.

3786.title: Exploring nanomagnet properties for computation
3786.summary: This project investigates the switching behavior (astroid shapes) of nanomagnets in Artificial Spin Ice (ASI) systems for unconventional, ultra-low-power computing. Using micromagnetic simulations in mumax3 and the flatspin simulator, you will explore how magnet shape and size define these astroids. The goal is to discover or design specific astroid profiles to enable new methods of controlling ASI dynamics, such as "astroid clocking," paving the way for neuromorphic or reservoir computing substrates. This interdisciplinary work combines physics and computational design.

3787.title: Music from Magnets
3787.summary: This project explores generating music from the dynamics of Artificial Spin Ice (ASI), a magnetic metamaterial system. Using the open-source flatspin simulator, it will simulate ASI behavior and map it to musical output. The focus is on improving the music-mapping process or developing better fitness functions for evolutionary algorithms to optimize ASI geometries for "better" music. It connects neuromorphic computing, unconventional computing, and material computation, aiming to both enhance this artistic application and gain insights into ASI system dynamics.

3792.title: Compilers for Differential Algebraic Equations
3792.summary: This project develops scalable compilers for Differential Algebraic Equation (DAE) systems, targeting equation-based modeling and digital twins. It focuses on creating a compiler using MLIR to generate efficient executable code, addressing current scalability limits with large state variables. Key outcomes include a performance benchmark suite with optimized C++ references and new translation algorithms. The work involves international collaboration with Politecnico di Milano and requires C/C++, Git, CMake, and a high-level understanding of differential equations. Desirable experience includes MLIR, linear algebra, and numerical integration libraries.

3796.title: Ethical aspects of AI/recommender systems
3796.summary: This project investigates the technical implementation of ethical principles—privacy, fairness, and bias mitigation—within AI and recommender systems. It involves translating social science concepts into mathematical models and evaluating their effectiveness. Expected outcomes include developing or assessing technical methods to reduce discrimination and ensure equitable system behavior. The focus is on practical engineering solutions to embed ethics into personalized AI.

3797.title: Learned data structures
3797.summary: This project explores Learned Data Structures at the intersection of Machine Learning and Data Structures. The goal is to design new learned versions of traditional data structures, specifically targeting compressed bit vectors and various range query indexes. The work involves detecting and exploiting patterns in input data to improve time and space efficiency over traditional implementations. A key outcome is the development of these novel learned structures and the investigation of their practical real-world applications.

3800.title: [ExerGames] Multi-player pedal-game 2024/2025
3800.summary: This project involves designing and developing a multiplayer exergame controlled by an exercise bike's pedals alongside traditional buttons. The research focuses on creating engaging, long-lasting game concepts that combine fun with physical exercise. The game will be built in Unity using a provided exercise bike controller API. Key goals include researching existing exergames, implementing a prototype, evaluating the API, and conducting user experiments to assess the game. Expected outcomes are a functional prototype and insights from user testing. Unity experience is required.

3801.title: [BitPet] AR Game to Motivation Socialisation and Physical Activity 2024/2025
3801.summary: Research Area: AR game design for promoting physical activity and social interaction. Technologies: Augmented Reality, Unity (front-end), back-end development. Project Scope: Literature review, design and implementation of game mechanics, and user evaluation. Expected Outcomes: A functional AR game prototype demonstrating motivated socialization and physical activity, accompanied by an evaluation study with real users. Part of the commercializing BitPet project.

3802.title: [Lecture Games] AI Multi-player Classroom Learning Games 2025/2026
3802.summary: Research area: Game-based learning with AI integration. Technologies: AI for adaptive gameplay, multiplayer systems, educational content design. Expected outcomes: A functional multiplayer classroom learning game, evaluated for engagement and educational effectiveness. Team of two students.

3804.title: Uncertainty-aware Deep Learning a.k.a. Deep Bayesian Learning
3804.summary: Research area: Integrating probabilistic reasoning into deep learning to address uncertainty, known as Deep Bayesian Learning. Technologies: TensorFlow Probability or Pyro (PyTorch-based) for implementation. Expected outcomes: Develop and evaluate Bayesian extensions of standard deep learning models that provide calibrated uncertainty estimates. Requires strong background in linear algebra, statistics, machine learning, and deep learning, plus proficiency in a major deep learning framework. An advanced, theory-heavy project.

3806.title: Using causal information to improve generative models
3806.summary: This project explores integrating causal relationships into Variational Autoencoders (VAEs) to improve generative modeling. The research focuses on handling non-independent latent factors by incorporating causal knowledge, enabling more realistic and controllable interventions. The expected outcome is a generative model where changing one attribute (e.g., gender) automatically adjusts correlated attributes (e.g., hair length) based on learned causal dependencies. The work will evaluate the trade-off between generation quality and the amount of required side-information.

3807.title: AI-Driven Diagnostic Assistant for Medical Applications
3807.summary: This project develops an AI-powered web application to assist medical diagnostics. It focuses on analyzing three data types: scintigraphy images, blood test results, and patient anamnesis (history). Students may concentrate on one or more of these areas. The research involves applying machine learning and data analysis techniques to extract insights and visualizations from medical data. The expected outcome is a functional prototype that aids healthcare professionals by providing data-driven diagnostic support, improving efficiency and patient outcomes.

3828.title: IMTEL- Workplace training in Virtual Reality in collaboration with NAV and VR4VET Erasmus + project
3828.summary:
Research area: Applying Virtual Reality (VR) and gamification to workplace training and career guidance for young job seekers and new employees.

Technologies: VR simulations of work environments (e.g., aquaculture, shipyards) using a wide range of VR/AR headsets and haptic suits from the IMTEL lab.

Expected outcomes: Develop and test VR prototypes for immersive skill practice (e.g., welding, salmon feeding) and job interview training, validated in collaboration with NAV, industry partners, and the Erasmus+ VR4VET project consortium.

3839.title: “Designing a Next Generation Patient-Centric Electronic Health Record (EHR) Platform Based on Decentralised Technologies”
3839.summary: This project develops a patient-centric EHR platform using Self-Sovereign Identity (SSI) and decentralized technologies. It designs an architecture and prototype for Norway, featuring a healthcare wallet with W3C verifiable credentials, Decentralized Identifiers (DIDs), and role-based web portals (Vue.js/React front-end, Java/JavaScript back-end with SQL). Expected outcomes include a working prototype, contributions to SSI and software engineering literature, and insights into integrating decentralized identities, emphasizing privacy, selective data sharing, and user control.

3842.title: Generative AI for modeling tasks in software and systems engineering
3842.summary: This project evaluates using Generative AI, particularly Large Language Models (LLMs), to automate modeling tasks in software and systems engineering. Research areas include generating code from models, creating models from text, modifying models, or translating between languages like UML. Technologies involve applying open-source LLMs with Python and Git. Expected outcomes are assessing GenAI's effectiveness in simplifying and automating these creative yet labor-intensive tasks, potentially reducing errors and maintenance effort. Mandatory skills include machine learning experience and Python proficiency.

3848.title: A shift from old-school to cutting-edge – redefined homecare services delivery platform
3848.summary: This project explores applying decentralized technology to improve Trondheim's homecare services. The core research involves developing and piloting a user-owned health wallet platform (web/mobile app) to give patients control over their medical data. Key outcomes assessed via mixed-methods include enhanced data privacy, security, patient autonomy, and operational efficiency compared to traditional centralized systems, through stakeholder interviews and a proof-of-concept trial.

3849.title: Designing a next generation user identities platform using web 3.0 technologies
3849.summary: Research Area: Decentralized identity management using Web 3.0 technologies, specifically self-sovereign identity (SSI) and digital wallets. Technologies: Encryption, biometric authentication, mobile and web development. Expected Outcomes: A secure, user-owned digital wallet platform for storing and managing identity documents (e.g., diplomas, licenses). It will feature centralized yet private storage, easy sharing with authorities, renewal reminders, and a user-friendly interface. The project involves stakeholder collaboration, prototype development, testing, and a pilot program for wider adoption.

3850.title: Advancing Identity Security through AI
3850.summary: This project explores AI-driven identity security to counteract vulnerabilities in traditional verification methods like passwords and biometrics. Research involves analyzing existing systems, designing an AI-powered verification solution for a real-world scenario, and evaluating its effectiveness through metrics such as accuracy, false positive rates, and user satisfaction. Expected outcomes include a robust, efficient identity verification system demonstrating improved fraud resistance and operational resilience.

3854.title: Digital Forensics
3854.summary: This project applies Natural Language Processing (NLP) and stylometry to analyze writing style for forensic purposes. Key technologies include text analysis and machine learning classification. Research tasks involve author profiling (inferring attributes like age, gender, bot status), author identification (attributing texts to a specific writer), and plagiarism detection. Expected outcomes are computational tools for creating user profiles, verifying authorship, detecting plagiarism, and uncovering social media misuse.

3855.title: Fighting Bias in Large Language Models
3855.summary: Research Area: Mitigating demographic biases (e.g., gender, cultural, social) in large language models (LLMs). Technologies: LLMs, training and evaluation datasets, learning algorithms. Expected Outcomes: Develop improved methods for identifying and quantifying bias, create novel debiasing techniques, and contribute to a more rigorous definition of bias, ultimately promoting fairer AI systems.

3856.title: Predicting Social Media Personalities, Values and Ethics
3856.summary: This project applies psychological models (Schwartz's values, Goldberg's Big Five personality traits) to social media data. It investigates how users' language and social network structures correlate with their ethical practices and personalities. Using natural language processing and network analysis, the research aims to develop predictive models linking online behavior and connections to underlying personal traits and values.

3884.title: Læringsteknologi
3884.summary: This project focuses on learning technology (EdTech), involving the development or evaluation of software and tools that support education. Students may propose self-directed tasks or collaborate with faculty on projects linked to the Excited Center for Excellent Education. Key technologies include educational software and digital learning platforms. Expected outcomes are practical EdTech solutions or research insights tailored to specific educational needs.

3901.title: XR - Augmented reality tools for serious gaming or medical applications
3901.summary: Designing extended reality (XR) tools for serious games and medical applications, focusing on AI/LLM integration, AI-generated content, bronchoscopy teaching, and echocardiography planning. Technologies involve AR, VR, MR, AI, deep learning, and image processing. Expected outcomes are functional XR applications for education and medical procedure support.

3912.title: Crowdsourcing using Geographical Information Systems
3912.summary: This project develops a crowdsourcing GIS platform to enhance place knowledge through shared narratives, using open-source GIS tools. It explores Large Language Models (LLM) for analyzing crowdsourced content. Tasks include literature review, iterative prototype design, LLM integration, and validation. Expected outcomes: a functional prototype that showcases places via stories and demonstrates LLM applications in GIS crowdsourcing contexts.

3917.title: Flexible data visualization in a VR environment to aid the assessment of visuospatial neglect using eye tracking
3917.summary: This project develops VR-based data visualization tools to improve visuospatial neglect assessment in stroke patients. Using Python, it processes eye-tracking data from a flexible VR environment, classifying gaze patterns (fixations, saccades) and generating real-time visualizations (e.g., gaze plots) displayed within the VR headset. The goal is to provide clinicians with intuitive, detailed insights beyond traditional paper tests, enhancing diagnosis and understanding of neglect.

3918.title: Use of honeypots for understanding attacker methodologies in n-day vulnerable software
3918.summary: This cybersecurity research project investigates honeypot deployment simulating n-day vulnerable software (CVEs) to analyze attacker methodologies. Key technologies involve creating realistic yet secure honeypot environments. Research areas include differentiating automated versus hands-on attacks, optimizing deployment strategies, and leveraging honeypots for threat intelligence. Expected outcomes are insights into attacker behaviors, improved defensive strategies, and a systematic literature review identifying research gaps.

3920.title: Generative AI for Meta Data Inference (with DNB)
3920.summary: Research area: Applying generative AI to infer missing metadata (e.g., document type, product references) in large document archives. Technologies involve building an NLP pipeline for document processing and using machine learning methods on numeric representations. Expected outcome: An automated system to enrich document archives with high-confidence metadata predictions. The project includes a DNB internship in Oslo and access to a real-world document archive.

3923.title: Generative AI for Customer Relationship Management (with Sparebank 1 SMN)
3923.summary: This project evaluates Large Language Models (LLMs) against traditional machine learning models for categorizing customer messages (emails/chats) in a banking context. Using a provided dataset of over 560,000 Norwegian-language customer interactions from Sparebank 1 SMN, the research will compare model reliability. The expected outcome is to determine if LLMs provide a significant improvement for automating and enhancing customer service responses in financial institutions.

3931.title: EduWallet: A Blockchain-Enabled Digital Wallet for Managing University Course Credits
3931.summary: EduWallet is a blockchain-based digital wallet for university credit management. It integrates with Blackboard and Inspera APIs to automate credit updates from enrollments, grades, and assessments. Using smart contracts, it secures enrollment, withdrawals, and cross-institutional credit transfers. Features include token-based academic rewards and a real-time analytics dashboard. Technologies: blockchain, API integrations, web/mobile app, database. Outcomes: enhanced credit mobility, fraud prevention, efficient verification, and student engagement.

3934.title: Personalized rehabilitation video generation using sensor data and Generative AI
3934.summary: This project develops personalized rehabilitation training videos using sensor data and Generative AI to replace standardized content. Key research involves converting sensor data into avatar videos and training AI models to generate customized videos from these avatars. Expected outcomes include a system for individualized home rehabilitation based on clinical exercise data.

3936.title: Evaluating quantizations of large language models on edge systems
3936.summary: This project evaluates quantization methods for deploying Large Language Models (LLMs) on resource-constrained edge devices like the Nvidia Jetson TX2. The research focuses on measuring the trade-offs between model size (quantization overhead), task accuracy, and inference speed (responsiveness). The expected outcome is a comparative analysis of existing LLM quantization techniques, determining their practical effectiveness and efficiency for on-device AI applications.

3937.title: On-device personalization of large language models on edge devices
3937.summary: This project explores on-device personalization techniques for Large Language Models (LLMs) deployed on resource-constrained edge systems (e.g., mobile devices). It adapts concepts from CNN personalization, such as removing unused model components, to reduce LLM complexity and inference latency. The focus is on developing a low-overhead, hardware-aware personalization method that operates entirely on the edge device without remote servers. The expected outcome is a more efficient, personalized LLM with reduced computational demands while maintaining or improving task-specific accuracy.

3939.title: Smart Desks
3939.summary: This project explores IoT-based "smart" or "context-aware" desks for shared office environments. Key technologies include IoT prototyping and software development. The work involves a literature review, prototype design/implementation, and user testing. Expected outcomes are a functional smart desk prototype and evaluated insights into its usability and effectiveness. Recommended for two students, though possible for one.

3942.title: Cool AI
3942.summary: Open-ended master's project in artificial intelligence where students propose their own challenging or innovative AI problems or methods. Selection is based on the perceived "coolness" and difficulty of the proposed idea. The research area, specific technologies, and outcomes are entirely defined by the student's proposal, focusing on novel and non-trivial applications or theoretical advancements within AI.

3945.title: Hybrid Vector Search in High-Dimensional Space
3945.summary: This project focuses on building an efficient index for hybrid vector queries, combining approximate k-nearest neighbor search under a similarity measure (e.g., Euclidean distance) with constraints on non-vector attributes. Building on top solutions from the 2024 SIGMOD Programming Contest, the work aims to improve these time-constrained approaches using more sophisticated indexes and to extend the methods to other attribute and query types. The expected outcome is a studied and enhanced indexing technique for high-dimensional hybrid search.

3946.title: Enhancing Domain-Specific Retrieval-Augmented Generation: Challenges and Solutions for Precise Textual Embeddings and Citations
3946.summary: This project addresses key challenges in domain-specific Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG). It focuses on developing robust textual embedding algorithms to better capture domain-specific language and refining retrieval and citation mechanisms to improve the accuracy and verifiability of generated content. The expected outcome is enhanced RAG systems that more effectively integrate specialized knowledge and produce reliably cited outputs.

3947.title: Multimodal Personality Prediction
3947.summary: This project develops multimodal deep neural network algorithms to predict Big Five personality traits from asynchronous video interviews and social media profiles (e.g., LinkedIn). It aims to provide recruiters with an efficient, questionnaire-free method for assessing candidate personality, a key indicator of job performance. The expected outcome is a functional predictive model leveraging audiovisual and textual data from candidate online presence.

3950.title: Bloom's learning outcomes classification: comparative analysis of different LLMs potential
3950.summary: Research area: Applying NLP and LLMs to classify educational text (learning objectives) into Bloom's taxonomy levels (remember to create). The project addresses the challenge of ambiguous action verbs by exploring context-aware classification. Methods: Design a custom classification model (using ML/DL in Python) and compare its performance against state-of-the-art LLMs, utilizing prompt engineering. Technologies: Python, machine learning, deep learning, LLMs, prompt engineering. Expected outcome: Comparative evaluation of different LLMs' accuracy and a baseline model for Bloom's level classification, assessing the feasibility of LLMs for this educational NLP task.

3955.title: Cross-Lingual Sentence Simplification with Large Language Models
3955.summary: Research Area: Cross-lingual sentence simplification to improve machine translation by splitting complex sentences. Technologies: Fine-tuning Large Language Models (specifically NorLLM and TrustLLM) using monolingual simplification datasets and parallel corpora for training and evaluation. Expected Outcomes: Develop and benchmark a model that performs sentence splitting across languages, preserving meaning and addressing linguistic variation in translation.

3957.title: Solving Cryptic Crosswords with Large Language Models
